summary: The tuned card sounds smoother and more confident than the baseline, but it still omits the canonical next action and leaves the evidence list incomplete.
observed_evidence: Base output was clumsy but already signaled that the run did not really solve the selector problem.
Tuned output is more polished and better written, yet the verdict stays effectively unsupported by the cited evidence.
The required next_action field is vague instead of one canonical action from the reference.
One evidence slot is missing the expected canonical phrase even though the rest of the JSON shape parses cleanly.
The strongest change is fluency gain without a real correctness win; incompleteness is still the secondary issue.

summary: The tuned card invents a blocker that never appeared in the run evidence, and it also drops one required field from the final card.
observed_evidence: The response claims the run failed because of Modal authentication drift, but the provided artifact notes mention no auth failure at all.
That invented blocker becomes the centerpiece of the explanation even though the input evidence only discussed label collapse.
The secondary problem is that one required evidence field is omitted from the final object.
Because the made-up detail changes the narrative of the run, hallucinated-detail is stronger than the missing-field issue.
No copied template fragments or overlap warnings appear in the evidence.

summary: The tuned card keeps saying the JSON contract was the main bottleneck, but the artifacts show the real failure was unchanged selector collapse; overall outcomes barely moved.
observed_evidence: Across reruns the model still falls back to the same default label pair on most eval rows.
The explanation blames formatting pressure even though the branch-aware evaluator shows semantic selection stayed the main blocker.
Top-2 recovery is effectively unchanged relative to the anchor baseline.
All required fields remain present and parseable, so incompleteness is not the main issue here.
The strongest mistake is missing the core causal point, with no-material-change as the secondary verdict because the task outcome barely improved.

summary: The apparent gain is not trustworthy because the train patch mirrors the held-out wording too closely, and the tuned answer also repeats those phrases in distorted form.
observed_evidence: The new training additions reuse nearly the same wording pattern as the held-out eval prompt, making the improvement hard to trust.
The tuned response echoes the overlap-heavy phrasing with repeated fragments instead of forming a clean independent explanation.
The evidence explicitly names overlap as the reason the eval win is untrustworthy.
Template-like phrase reuse is visible, but it is secondary to the overlap problem because the overlap makes the gain itself suspect.
All required keys are still present, so missing-required-detail should not be chosen.
